page: Microsoft Azure D32pds_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DPDSv5 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores on an ARMv8 64-bit architecture, powered by an Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor operating at 3.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Additionally, the instance is equipped with 1229 GB of local SSD storage and is capable of utilizing Premium Storage.

Benchmark evaluations place the D32pds_v5 in the average performance tier for most standard operations. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance is recorded at 1822.59, while multi-core performance reaches 57203.87. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 2232.35 GB/sec, dropping to 166.64 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, and a write speed of 75.94 GB/sec. The system demonstrates solid database capabilities, achieving 21065.71 in PassMark database operations and processing 40.63 million Redis SET operations per second.

Tradeoffs are evident in specialized workloads. Due to its ARM architecture, the server lacks x86-specific instruction sets such as AVX, AVX-512, and AMX. Consequently, large-scale machine learning tasks perform poorly; LLM inference speeds for a 70B model drop to 3.6 tokens/sec for prompt processing and 2.31 tokens/sec for text generation. However, the server remains highly cost-efficient for standard multi-threaded applications, software compilation, and static web serving, where it achieves a throughput of 31.62 GB/sec. This makes it a strong fit for general-purpose workloads, web serving, and database hosting.
description: Microsoft Azure D32pds_v5 is an ARM-based general-purpose virtual machine powered by a 32-core Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz. It features 128.0 GB of RAM and 1229 GB of local SSD storage, supported by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Benchmark data indicates average multi-core performance with a stress-ng score of 57203.87 and a static web serving throughput of 31.62 GB/sec. While the instance lacks x86 vector extensions like AVX-512, it delivers efficient database operations, processing 40.63 million Redis SET operations per second. The server is optimized for general-purpose workloads, software compilation, and database hosting, offering a balanced resource profile without the overhead of specialized x86 instruction sets.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32pds_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine featuring 32 Ampere Neoverse-N1 cores, 128 GB RAM, and 1229 GB local storage, optimized for general-purpose and database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32pds_v5 is a 32-core ARM64 virtual machine with 128 GB RAM and 1229 GB local storage, built for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered virtual machine offering balanced compute, memory, and local storage for general-purpose applications and database hosting.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated Ampere Neoverse-N1 ARMv8 cores running at 3.0 GHz
128.0 GB of system memory and 1229 GB of local SSD storage
Delivers 31.62 GB/sec static web serving throughput
Processes 40.63 million Redis SET operations per second
Lacks x86 vector extensions, resulting in poor large LLM inference performance
Best suited for general-purpose applications, software compilation, and database hosting
